iterations.title: Plan the review output lifecycle: resolve the require-vs-spawn fork, slice G2+G6 execution
P1 (G6): ingest auto-captures review_gaps as open gap records
P2 (G2): ingest requires an owning workstream (-w or --spawn)
P3: closeout - dogfood the lifecycle on a real review, resolve G2+G6, retro, close

plan.sketch: P1 (G6): ingest auto-captures review_gaps as open gap records via gap.add_gap (context = review slug + why_missed + proposed_change); the printed queue plan drops its 'lume gap add' lines; --json carries the captured gap ids; tests cover capture, context tagging, and the slimmer queue plan; docs (SKILL.md, plugin/README) updated

P2 (G2): ingest requires an owning workstream - '-w <active-ws>' attributes the review to it, '--spawn' creates review-<date>-NN (objective from provenance + finding counts, no iteration opened); refuses with a named error when neither is given; the review store doc and --json carry the workstream id; tests cover -w, --spawn, refusal, and no-self-approve; docs updated

P3: dogfood on the next real 'lume review' run (ingest under a spawned workstream, gaps auto-captured), resolve G2+G6 via gap resolve -w 0020, retro (did the lifecycle change buy its cost?), lume close

workstream.title: Review output is born in a workstream; review_gaps auto-captured on ingest
